question: The name 'Rolling Stones' was taken from a song by which blues musician?
At which 1969 free festival did a Hells Angels member fatally stab an audience member during a Rolling Stones performance?
The Rolling Stones were pressured to change the lyrics of which song for their 1967 appearance on The Ed Sullivan Show?
The Rolling Stones held a free concert in Hyde Park in July 1969 shortly after Brian Jones died. What did Mick Jagger release in his memory?
The Rolling Stones' first UK number one single (1964) was a cover of a Bobby Womack song. Which song was it?
Brian Jones purchased Cotchford Farm in 1968 — previously the home of which beloved British author?
Who was the Rolling Stones' first manager, credited with crafting their rebellious anti-Beatles image?
What was the Rolling Stones' original UK record label?
'Wild Horses' was first recorded by which artist before the Rolling Stones released it on Sticky Fingers?
The Rolling Stones founded their own label in 1971. What was it called?
What was the name of the suppressed 1972 Robert Frank documentary that followed the Stones on their North American tour?
The Rolling Stones filmed a lavish 1968 TV special — never broadcast at the time — featuring The Who and John Lennon. What was it called?
The Rolling Stones took their name from a Muddy Waters song called 'Rollin' ___'.
The Rolling Stones' iconic 'tongue and lips' logo was designed by graphic artist John ___.
The disastrous 1969 concert at ___ Speedway, intended to be a West Coast Woodstock, ended in tragedy.
The Rolling Stones' first manager was Andrew Loog ___.
Brian Jones purchased Cotchford Farm, the former home of Winnie the Pooh author A.A. ___.
The Rolling Stones' original UK label was ___ Records, which they had a famously bitter split with.
The Rolling Stones' 1968 unaired TV special featured John Lennon and The Who, and was called 'The Rolling Stones Rock and Roll ___'.
The suppressed 1972 Robert Frank tour documentary is titled '___ Blues'.
What was the name of the 1969 free concert where a Hells Angels member fatally stabbed an audience member during the Rolling Stones' set?
Name the Rolling Stones' first UK number one single — a 1964 cover of a song originally recorded by Bobby Womack's group The Valentinos.
Who was the Rolling Stones' first manager, the young impresario who deliberately positioned them as the rebellious alternative to The Beatles?
Brian Jones bought Cotchford Farm in 1968. Which author had lived there and written his most famous works in its grounds?
Name the band that first recorded 'Wild Horses' — featuring Gram Parsons — before the Rolling Stones released it on Sticky Fingers.
